# AUGMANITAI

> A curated terminology framework for phenomena at the human–AI interface. 1000 terms, informed by ISO 704 / ISO 1087 / ISO 30042 (ISO-inspired, not ISO-certified), published on Zenodo under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. Author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796).

## Ethical disclaimer (§1–§20)

AUGMANITAI is purely descriptive linguistic-terminological research.
- §1 Descriptive, not normative.
- §11 Non-peer-reviewed. AUGMANITAI is a longitudinal n=1 terminological priority deposit, not a peer-reviewed publication. See METHODOLOGY.md §2.0.
- §13 No efficacy claims, not a substitute for professional advice.
- §7 18+ only.
- §20 Must not be used as diagnostic label, medical/psychological classification, legal categorization, HR screening, credit scoring, insurance assessment, state surveillance, or profiling mechanism.

Full disclaimer: `DISCLAIMER.md` in the repository root.

## Canonical resources

- Zenodo DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19481331 — canonical published version
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0006-3773-7796 — author identity

## What AUGMANITAI is

AUGMANITAI is a research terminology and a priority-establishing terminological deposit. Its core deliverable is the 1000-Term Compendium, the curated output of a longitudinal n=1 observation of daily human–LLM interaction over approximately nine months, formalized as an RDF knowledge graph with OWL, SKOS, SKOS-XL, OntoLex-Lemon, SHACL, and JSON-LD serializations, plus a TBX export for terminology management systems.

The priority claim covers the schema as a whole — the selection, definition, relation structure, and serialization — not the invention of each individual term. Some terms are imported from prior literature, some are re-formulations, some are newly introduced. See METHODOLOGY.md §3 for the origin categories.

It is a research instrument, not a product, not a methodology, and not a manifesto. Terms are tools for observation.

## Standards the framework is informed by

Per §16 of the Ethical Disclaimer, AUGMANITAI is ISO-inspired, not ISO-certified. The standards listed below informed the methodology; listing them does not assert certification or conformance.

- ISO 704:2022 — Terminology work: principles and methods (informed by)
- ISO 1087:2019 — Terminology work: vocabulary (informed by)
- ISO 30042:2019 — TermBase eXchange (TBX) (informed by)
- W3C SKOS, SKOS-XL, OntoLex-Lemon, SHACL, JSON-LD 1.1
- W3C Verifiable Credentials 2.0 (for provenance)

## Repository structure

- `ontology/` — core OWL, SKOS, OntoLex-Lemon, SKOS-XL, JSON-LD, SHACL, TBX
- `protocols/` — MCP, A2A, ANP, AG-UI protocol adapters
- `provenance/` — C2PA, SPDX 3.0 AI Profile, CycloneDX 1.6 ML-BOM, W3C VC 2.0
- `graphrag-variants/` — six GraphRAG integration examples
- `demo-swarms/` — multi-agent reference examples (with mock LLMs)
- `governance/` — Code of Conduct, CLA, DCO, Security, Maintainers, Roadmap
- `licensing/` — dual-license files, SPDX, PATENT, TRADEMARK, NOTICE
- `DISCLAIMER.md` — Ethical Disclaimer §1–§20 (bilingual DE/EN)
